Further investigations, including genetic testing and imaging studies (e.g., cranial CT), are warranted to determine the precise diagnosis and associated risk factors.
116: Patient is a 2-year-old male who presents with a constellation of congenital anomalies.  He is noted to have tall stature, disproportionately large for gestational age, and significant hemihypertrophy affecting the right side of his body.  Physical examination reveals a wide mouth, macroglossia, prominent occiput, coarse facial features, mandibular prognathism, and an abnormality of the right earlobe.  Proptosis and midface retrusion are also present.  He has a congenital diaphragmatic hernia on the left side, requiring ongoing respiratory support.  The patient exhibits signs of accelerated skeletal maturation and redundant skin folds.  Palpation reveals an enlarged kidney and palpable splenomegaly.  He has a history of omphalocele and umbilical hernia, repaired in infancy.  Additionally, he reports episodes of hypoglycemia requiring frequent feeding adjustments.  Laboratory investigations reveal elevated alpha-fetoprotein levels and hypercalciuria.  Ultrasound reveals multiple small medullary renal cysts, nephrocalcinosis, and an enlarged kidney.  A nephropathy is suspected, and further imaging and genetic studies are indicated.  Other noted findings include a postauricular pit, a posterior helix pit, polyhydramnios, an umbilical hernia, and a subchorionic septal cyst noted on prenatal ultrasound.  He exhibits significant feeding difficulties and sleep apnea, impacting his nutritional intake and overall well-being. He has a history of premature birth and exocrine pancreatic insufficiency.  Genitourinary examination shows ureteral duplication and vesicoureteral reflux. He has a wide anterior fontanel and small fontanelles, and a cleft palate. The patient also has evidence of otosclerosis, possibly contributing to hearing impairment, and a previously noted facial hemangioma.  He exhibits delayed gross motor development and has a history of cryptorchidism.  He is noted to have multiple congenital anomalies of the cardiovascular system, including a hypertrophic cardiomyopathy and cardiomegaly.  He has a history of neonatal hypoglycemia.  A neoplasm is suspected.  Further investigation for genetic syndromes, including possible Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome, is warranted.  A full developmental assessment and ongoing monitoring for potential malignancies (e.g., nephroblastoma, rhabdomyosarcoma, neuroblastoma, adrenocortical carcinoma) are recommended.
112: "Patient is a 32-year-old male who presents with progressive fatigue, unexplained weight fluctuations despite maintaining a consistent diet, and a noticeable reduction in muscle mass.  He reports a history of frequent, non-specific aches and pains throughout his body, including episodes of arthralgia.  He describes an increasing difficulty concentrating and experiencing periods of mental fogginess.  He also notes a history of prolonged periods of feeling cold, despite warm environmental conditions.  Laboratory investigations reveal elevated serum triglycerides and cholesterol levels, with mildly elevated fasting blood glucose.  A comprehensive metabolic panel demonstrates impaired glucose tolerance and insulin resistance.  Physical examination reveals a consistent short stature, measured at 1.55m, well below the expected height percentile for his age.  He denies any history of significant trauma or prolonged illness.  Radiographic evaluation shows diminished bone density, characteristic of osteopenia, despite his age.  His medical history is otherwise unremarkable except for a reported history of mild iron deficiency anemia in adolescence.  The patient’s family history is significant for a maternal aunt diagnosed with hypothyroidism and a paternal grandfather with a history of diabetes mellitus type II.  Given the constellation of symptoms and laboratory findings, a diagnosis of possible growth hormone deficiency and associated metabolic syndrome is being considered."
53: Patient is a 10-year-old male presenting with progressive macrocephaly, frontal bossing, and significant bone pain, particularly in the long bones.  He reports recurrent fractures, primarily in the long bones of his extremities, with minimal trauma.  Physical examination reveals generalized osteosclerosis, abnormal metaphyseal morphology in the long bones, and short distal phalanges of the fingers.  Arthritis and multiple joint dislocations, particularly in the knees and ankles (genu valgum), are noted.  His stature is markedly short for his age.  He exhibits cranial nerve paralysis affecting the facial and optic nerves, leading to facial palsy and optic atrophy, respectively.  Visual impairment and hearing impairment are also present.  He is anemic and thrombocytopenic, with abnormal leukocyte morphology evident in blood tests.  Laboratory results are significant for hypocalcemia and low levels of various bone-related biomarkers.  Radiographic studies reveal osteomyelitis of the mandible, generalized osteosclerosis, and an abnormality of epiphyseal and metaphyseal morphology throughout the skeleton, with evidence of avascular necrosis in multiple joints.  Abnormal dentition, including carious teeth and tooth abscesses, is present.  He also displays scoliosis, hydrocephalus, and the presence of a large number of bone fractures throughout his development.  Biopsy of bone marrow reveals an abnormality of multiple cell lineages.  His medical history includes recurrent episodes of fatigue and generalized pain.  The patient also demonstrates an abnormality of the metacarpal morphology and an osteosclerosis of the base of the skull.  He has experienced several episodes of cranial nerve compression, likely contributing to his neurological dysfunction.  Given the constellation of findings, a diagnosis of a severe, systemic skeletal dysplasia is suspected.
50: Patient is a 3-year-old male who presents with a complex constellation of congenital anomalies.  He exhibits a cleft palate and cleft upper lip, along with microcephaly and a short philtrum.  Prominent ear protrusion is noted.  Significant ophthalmological findings include retinal detachment, chorioretinal coloboma, microphthalmia, optic disc coloboma, nystagmus, and optic atrophy.  He has experienced delayed puberty, and, more recently, precocious puberty.  Skeletal anomalies include bifid ribs, rib fusion, missing ribs, and plagiocephaly, along with hip dysplasia.  He also presents with a sparse lateral eyebrow and supernumerary ribs.  Neurological examination reveals hypotonia, progressing to spasticity in recent months, and hypertonia,  pachygyria, and partial agenesis of the corpus callosum.  He has been diagnosed with moderate global developmental delay, with a severe intellectual disability, and exhibits an EEG abnormality.  Scoliosis is apparent.  His physical examination reveals multiple lipomas and an abnormality of skin pigmentation.  He has a history of feeding difficulties in infancy and currently experiences constipation, gastroesophageal reflux, and malabsorption, with a suspected hiatus hernia.  Imaging studies reveal ventriculomegaly and polymicrogyria.  He was recently diagnosed with a hepatoblastoma.  Vertebral anomalies, including block vertebrae and butterfly vertebrae, are also present.  Hemiplegia/hemiparesis is noted on the right side. Aplasia/hypoplasia of the cerebellum is suspected.  An abnormality of retinal pigmentation is documented.  A recent colonoscopy revealed intestinal polyposis.  He has small hands.  His medical history includes multiple hospitalizations for infantile spasms and recurrent respiratory infections.
52: Patient is a 3-year-old male who presents with a constellation of congenital anomalies.  He exhibits significant failure to thrive and intrauterine growth retardation, with current weight and height below the 3rd percentile.  Physical examination reveals brachycephaly, a round face with coarse facial features, a pointed chin, and a short philtrum.  Hypertelorism, a prominent feature, is accompanied by downslanted palpebral fissures and deeply set eyes.  The patient also demonstrates a protruding ear and strabismus.  Micrognathia is evident.  Keratoconus and abnormal pupil morphology are noted.  Clinodactyly of the 5th finger is present, along with a long nose.  Peripheral pulmonary artery stenosis is suspected, contributing to the ongoing respiratory distress.  Abnormalities of the vertebral bodies, including a butterfly vertebral arch and spina bifida occulta, are apparent on radiographs.  Abnormal rib morphology is also observed.  The patient exhibits delayed skeletal maturation, with hypoplasia of the ulna and short distal phalanges of the fingers.  A flat face is noted along with telangiectasia of the skin.  His cryptorchidism remains untreated, necessitating ongoing follow-up.  Abnormality of the ureter is suggested by ultrasound findings, warranting further investigation.  Nephrotic syndrome is suspected, evidenced by significant proteinuria.  Elevated blood pressure suggests hypertension.  Delayed puberty is also noted.  Corneal dystrophy is present, requiring ophthalmologic management.  The patient displays mild intellectual disability and specific learning disabilities.  Hepatomegaly and cholestasis are confirmed by laboratory testing, along with reduced number of intrahepatic bile ducts.  Further evaluation reveals a ventricular septal defect and an atrial septal defect.  Renal hypoplasia/aplasia is a significant concern, contributing to the potential nephrotic syndrome.  The patient's history includes a documented history of significant intrauterine growth retardation and failure to thrive.
14: Patient is a 32-year-old male who presents with a progressive neurological deterioration, marked by ataxia, dysarthria, and dysmetria, contributing to a broad-based gait and gait ataxia.  He reports chronic diarrhea, steatorrhea, and significant weight loss, leading to failure to thrive.  He also experiences recurrent vomiting episodes.  Physical examination reveals a characteristic steppage gait, pes cavus, and talipes equinovarus.  Neurological examination demonstrates an abnormality of the nervous system, including areflexia, impaired vibratory sensation, impaired distal proprioception, and a positive Romberg sign.  He also has a history of progressive visual loss, with nyctalopia, color vision defect, and scotoma.  Fundoscopic examination reveals hypopigmentation of the fundus.  He reports significant myalgia and distal lower limb muscle weakness.  Laboratory findings are notable for acanthocytosis, fat malabsorption, and abnormal circulating apolipoprotein concentrations, along with low levels of vitamins E, A, and D.  He has anemia with reticulocytosis, hyperbilirubinemia, hypoalbuminemia, hypocholesterolemia, low HDL and LDL cholesterol concentrations, and hypotriglyceridemia.  Elevated hepatic transaminases suggest hepatic steatosis, and the presence of cirrhosis and hepatic fibrosis is suspected based on the patient’s clinical presentation.  Hepatomegaly is also evident on palpation.  Furthermore, ophthalmological examination reveals ptosis and ophthalmoplegia.  Prolonged prothrombin time suggests an abnormal bleeding tendency.  He also has signs of hypothyroidism, including keratoconjunctivitis sicca.  X-rays reveal osteopenia and kyphoscoliosis.  He reports progressive respiratory difficulties, potentially indicating respiratory failure. Corneal ulcerations are present.  A comprehensive review of systems reveals congestive heart failure with cardiomegaly.  His medical history includes a diagnosis of rod-cone dystrophy.  Given the constellation of findings, including the progressive neurologic and ocular deterioration, the abnormal lipid profile, and the significant hepatic involvement, a diagnosis of a severe, multi-system disorder consistent with a specific genetic condition that causes combined deficiencies in fat-soluble vitamins, is highly suspected.
195: Patient is a 3-year-old male who presents with a constellation of congenital anomalies.  He reports no significant subjective complaints.  Physical examination reveals a preauricular skin tag on the right, a preauricular pit on the left, and palpable hypertelorism.  Downslanted palpebral fissures are noted, along with microphthalmia and bilateral chorioretinal and iris coloboma.  A palpable anal atresia is documented.  The patient also demonstrates significant hypotonia, short stature, and mild intellectual disability, suggestive of intrauterine growth retardation.  Examination reveals abnormal rib morphology and hip dysplasia.  A pronounced hydronephrosis is present on ultrasound, and renal hypoplasia is suspected. The kidneys are noted to have an abnormal localization.  Cardiovascular system morphology appears abnormal on echocardiogram.  Further examination reveals an abnormality of the genital system, requiring further evaluation.  Hearing impairment is suspected based on developmental delay and absent response to acoustic stimuli.  Developmental milestones are notably delayed.  No specific laboratory abnormalities are notable at this time.  His medical history is otherwise unremarkable, though a detailed family history remains to be elucidated.
289: Patient is a 2-year-old male who presents with a constellation of developmental abnormalities, consistent with a suspected genetic syndrome.  He demonstrates significant intrauterine growth retardation, evidenced by micromelia, neonatal short-limb short stature, and a short thorax.  Physical examination reveals narrow chest, genu valgum, and cubitus valgus.  Polydactyly is noted in both hands and feet.  Abnormal fingernail morphology and hypoplastic toenails are observed.  Nail dysplasia and an abnormality of the hair are also present.  The patient exhibits delayed skeletal maturation, with delayed eruption of teeth and hypodontia/microdontia.  Abnormal oral mucosa morphology is noted, along with an abnormality of the oral frenulum.  Conical incisors are present. A thin vermilion border is also apparent.  Dextrocardia and situs inversus totalis are evident on cardiac imaging.  An atrioventricular canal defect, ventricular septal defect, and atrial septal defect are confirmed by echocardiogram.  Abnormal cardiovascular system morphology is indicated, along with an abnormality of the heart valve.  Cryptorchidism and epispadias are identified.  The patient exhibits an abnormality of the skeletal system, including capitate-hamate fusion, synostosis of carpal bones, and an abnormality of pelvic girdle bone morphology.  Renal hypoplasia/aplasia and an abnormality of the ureter are evident on imaging.  Hydroureter is also observed.  Abnormalities of the female internal genitalia (which is important to confirm in any prepubertal male patient and would likely be abnormal) are noted.  Aplasia/hypoplasia of the lungs and emphysema are apparent on pulmonary imaging. A history of acute leukemia is noted with abnormal bone marrow cell morphology.  Intellectual disability is suspected given delayed developmental milestones, and failure to thrive is ongoing.  A significant abnormality of the dentition is evident, including delayed eruption and abnormal morphology.  The patient also displays an abnormality of the nail, an abnormal hair quantity, and features consistent with severe developmental delays.
474: Patient is a 2-year-old male who presents with a constellation of developmental abnormalities.  He exhibits significant short stature, estimated at the 3rd percentile for age and sex.  Physical examination reveals a narrow chest, abnormal rib morphology, and an abnormal sternum.  Clavicle abnormalities are also noted.  He has brachydactyly, postaxial hand polydactyly, and toe syndactyly.  A short foot, with postaxial foot polydactyly, and micromelia are also present.  The patient's metaphyseal morphology is abnormal. The pelvic girdle bones also demonstrate abnormal morphology.  His skeletal dysplasia is visually evident.  Feeding difficulties were present from infancy.  His respiratory status is concerning; he demonstrates respiratory insufficiency and aplasia/hypoplasia of the lungs.  He has been receiving ongoing supplemental oxygen support.  The patient also exhibits an abnormality of liver function, confirmed by lab testing.  Abnormal retinal pigmentation is noted.  Renal insufficiency is evident on recent blood work, with impaired glomerular filtration rate and electrolyte imbalances.  Further investigation reveals a diagnosis consistent with nephronophthisis and nephropathy.  His medical history reveals no known previous episodes of kidney disease.
861: A 2-year-old male presents with a constellation of congenital anomalies.  He reports significant feeding difficulties since infancy, necessitating specialized feeding techniques and supplemental nutrition.  Physical examination reveals a small scrotum, with cryptorchidism, and a hypoplastic penis.  A rectovaginal fistula is noted, along with a wide mouth, narrow mouth, and significant glossoptosis.  Cleft palate and cleft upper lip are evident, coupled with a high palate and brachycephaly.  Hypertelorism and preauricular skin tags are present, along with choanal atresia.  Bilateral microphthalmia and cataracts are observed, accompanied by blepharospasm and abnormalities of dental enamel.  The patient also exhibits dysmorphic facial features including malar flattening, retrognathia, hypoplasia of the maxilla, and micrognathia.  Downslanted palpebral fissures and an abnormal facial shape are noted.  The patient demonstrates a globally delayed developmental trajectory, consistent with failure to thrive.  Multiple enchondromatosis is suspected based on radiographic findings.  He exhibits a patent ductus arteriosus, requiring ongoing medical management.  A tracheoesophageal fistula is suspected due to recurrent aspiration episodes.  Respiratory insufficiency necessitates respiratory support.  Dysphasia and delayed speech and language development are evident.  Thyroid hypoplasia is noted, and abnormalities of the adrenal glands are suspected.  Significant hypoplasia of the thymus is confirmed by imaging studies.  Abnormality of the vertebral column and skeletal dysplasia are confirmed by radiographic evaluation, further complicated by abnormalities of bone mineral density and hypoplasia of the zygomatic bone.  The patient presents with open bite malocclusion and a short face, with midface retrusion and an abnormal dentition. A low anterior hairline and abnormality of the outer ear are observed, alongside posteriorly rotated ears.  Abnormalities of the middle ear and a conductive hearing impairment, coupled with atresia of the external auditory canal, further complicate his audiological status.  His nasal bridge is wide, and strabismus and visual impairment are noted.  The presence of absent eyelashes, iris coloboma, and eyelid coloboma are also present.  Reduced number of teeth and a narrow internal auditory canal are noted.  A preauricular hair displacement and branchial fistula are detected.  Abnormal lacrimal duct morphology and abnormal cardiovascular system morphology are evident on echocardiography and other imaging studies.  A significant facial cleft is present.  Finally, an encephalocele is present.
199:
Patient is a 3-year-old male presenting with a complex constellation of congenital anomalies.  He was born prematurely at 32 weeks gestation with intrauterine growth retardation and significant feeding difficulties in infancy.  He experienced severe postnatal growth retardation.  Physical examination reveals a multitude of dysmorphic features, including a high palate, thin vermilion border, brachycephaly, microcephaly, low anterior hairline, long philtrum, micrognathia, atresia of the external auditory canal, anteverted nares, short neck, long eyelashes, thick eyebrow, synophrys, a low posterior hairline, generalized hirsutism, highly arched eyebrow, downturned corners of mouth, short nose with depressed nasal bridge, short stature, and microcornea.  There is also evidence of toe syndactyly, short foot, and short 1st metacarpal.  His eyelashes are curly, and he exhibits proximal placement of the thumb.  Delayed eruption of teeth and widely spaced teeth are also noted.  The patient displays bilateral single transverse palmar creases.  Macrotia, cleft palate, and choanal atresia are present.  His genitalia exhibit hypoplasia of the penis, cryptorchidism, and hypospadias, along with hypoplastic labia majora.  He has a short 1st metacarpal, and his voice is abnormally low-pitched.

Examination reveals conductive and sensorineural hearing impairment.  He has a history of multiple episodes of feeding difficulties, and is diagnosed with gastroesophageal reflux.  He demonstrates delayed skeletal maturation and micromelia.  There are signs of blepharitis, ptosis, myopia, phthisis bulbi, and strabismus.  Glaucoma, cataracts, and nystagmus are also present.  Hypertonia and hypotonia alternate, with episodes of joint stiffness and cutis marmorata.

Neurologically, intellectual disability is severe, and there is a history of attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), obsessive-compulsive behavior, and autism spectrum disorder.  He exhibits abnormalities of speech and vocalization, as well as sleep abnormalities.  He displays multiple cardiac anomalies, including a ventricular septal defect, atrial septal defect, and abnormal cardiovascular morphology.  There is evidence of intestinal malrotation, volvulus, and congenital diaphragmatic hernia.  His renal system is affected, with multicystic kidney dysplasia and vesicoureteral reflux, as well as renal insufficiency.  He displays hip dysplasia, and hip dislocations are noted.  The patient exhibits radioulnar synostosis, elbow dislocation, and clinodactyly of the 5th finger.

A detailed history reveals prenatal movement abnormality.  The patient has a short stature, and his uterus demonstrates an abnormality.  A complete workup shows primary amenorrhea and delayed puberty.  He experienced a seizure, and ventriculomegaly and cerebral cortical atrophy are noted on imaging studies.  Additional findings include abnormalities of the ulna, talipes, and truncal obesity.  He displays aplasia/hypoplasia of the cerebellum and peripheral neuropathy.  Increased nuchal translucency was detected prenatally.  He exhibits oligodactyly.  His medical history includes a documented abnormality of the ulna, delayed skeletal maturation, and severe postnatal growth retardation.  The patient's prenatal ultrasound demonstrated a significant increase in nuchal translucency.
2162: A 2-year-old male patient presents with a constellation of congenital anomalies.  He exhibits cryptorchidism, bilateral cleft lip and palate, and an abnormality of the urinary system, likely contributing to his proteinuria.  He has a history of hydrocephalus requiring shunt placement, and significant microcephaly, alongside macrocephaly, with abnormal skull morphology.  Epicanthus, a broad philtrum, hypertelorism, and macrotia are prominent facial features.  The patient also displays a depressed nasal tip, choanal atresia, a depressed nasal ridge, and anosmia.  Anteverted nares and a short neck are present.  He is noted to have retinopathy, deeply set eyes, ptosis, and anophthalmia.  A chorioretinal coloboma and microphthalmia are also observed.  Thick eyebrows, blepharophimosis, upslanted palpebral fissures, and hypotelorism are apparent.  Iris coloboma, optic atrophy, and synophrys are present.  He has a congenital diaphragmatic hernia, requiring ongoing respiratory support.  A history of diabetes mellitus is reported, along with anterior hypopituitarism and suspected panhypopituitarism.  Diabetes insipidus is a concern.  Brachydactyly and hand polydactyly are observed.  The patient has experienced multiple seizure events and displays hypotonia with subsequent spasticity.  Global developmental delay is apparent, with a history of failure to thrive in infancy.  Omphalocele was present at birth.  A ventricular septal defect, tetralogy of Fallot, and abnormal pulmonary valve and aortic morphology are noted.  Abnormalities of the spleen are suspected.  The patient also exhibits talipes.  Hypoglycemia, abnormal facial shape, a deep philtrum, frontal bossing, and constipation are further complicating factors.  Gastroesophageal reflux and chorea are reported.  Encephalocele is suspected.  Respiratory insufficiency requires ongoing intervention.  Evidence of abnormal neuronal migration is possible.  A highly arched eyebrow, scoliosis, hyponatremia, and an abnormal form of the vertebral bodies are present.  Hyposmia and a flat occiput are also noted.  Joint hyperflexibility is observed. A single median maxillary incisor is present. Aplasia/hypoplasia of the lungs, cerebellum, and corpus callosum are apparent.  External ear malformation and hypoplasia of the penis are also noted.  Feeding difficulties in infancy and difficulties with dentition, (reduced number of teeth) are also present.  An abnormality of the antihelix and a branchial anomaly are suspected. Cyclopia, aplasia/hypoplasia involving the nose, spinal dysraphism, and a spinal cord tumor are potential contributors to his clinical presentation.  Hypoplasia of the zygomatic bone and intestinal atresia are also noted.  Arrhythmias are concerning.  Overall, there is a suspicion of an underlying chromosomal abnormality or genetic disorder, given the wide range of abnormalities affecting multiple organ systems and with abnormal nervous system morphology.
87: Patient is a 2-year-old male who presents with a constellation of congenital anomalies.  He reports significant feeding difficulties since infancy, requiring specialized nutritional support.
